title: Filipino-Style Ginataang Bilo-Bilo
description: A traditional Filipino dessert made with sweet bilo-bilo balls, coconut milk and various fruits. This recipe is a twist on the classic by adding a caramelized sugar crust to give it a crunchy texture.

ingredients.name: Sweet potato bilo-bilo balls
ingredients.notes: You can use store-bought or homemade bilo-bilo balls

ingredients.name: Coconut milk
ingredients.notes: Full-fat coconut milk for a richer flavor

ingredients.name: Date syrup
ingredients.notes: Or substitute with honey or maple syrup

ingredients.name: Charred sugar
ingredients.notes: To make charred sugar, caramelize 1/4 cup of sugar in a pan over medium heat until golden brown

ingredients.name: Crispy plantains
ingredients.notes: Slice plantains thinly and fry until crispy

ingredients.name: Night market-style jackfruit
ingredients.notes: Canned jackfruit or use fresh jackfruit and cook until tender

instructions: In a large saucepan, combine coconut milk, date syrup, and charred sugar. Heat over medium heat, stirring constantly until the sugar has dissolved.
Add the bilo-bilo balls to the saucepan and cook for 5 minutes, or until they have absorbed most of the liquid.
Add the crispy plantains and night market-style jackfruit to the saucepan. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the flavors have melded together.
Transfer the mixture to individual serving bowls and serve warm or at room temperature.
